summary: Recorded gate evidence genuinely demonstrates all five acceptance criteria and spec clauses S-001..S-005. Content-binding hashes for docs/architecture/target-model.json, src/architecture-conformance.js, src/cli.js, and test/architecture-conformance.test.js all match the current working-tree files, and the recorded HEAD (8a05082) equals current HEAD, so the evidence is fresh under the content-surface freshness policy. The dry-run contract holds: cli.js computes exitCode = strict && violation_count>0 ? 2 : 0, the real-repo integration run reported 66 violations with exit 0, and runArchitectureConformance/renderConformanceMarkdown are referenced only from cli.js with no gate_status/pr-prepare coupling (Story Non-Goal respected). The change is purely additive (new module, new subcommand, new doc/model/tests). Two low-severity coverage nuances remain but do not block: the loadGraph fallbacks for the `edges` array and `type` relation are never exercised (real graph.json and every fixture use `links`+`relation`), and the unit test asserts only artifact path strings, not rendered markdown content (markdown correctness confirmed by the integration observation and manual inspection of conformance.md).

findings.detail: src/architecture-conformance.js loadGraph accepts two edge-shape variants (links|edges array, relation|type key), but neither the 12 unit fixtures nor the real repo graph.json exercise the `edges`/`type` branch — the real Graphify output uses `links`+`relation`. The alternate edge-shape input path is defensive but unverified. Non-blocking since the occurring shape is fully covered.

test 'cli conformance writes json and markdown artifacts' asserts only result.artifacts.json/.markdown path strings, not renderConformanceMarkdown output content. The user-read markdown surface (advisory notice, violation groupings) is validated only by the integration observation and manual inspection of .vibepro/architecture/conformance/conformance.md, not by an automated assertion.

inspection_summary: Read the review request and both mandatory lenses; inspected src/architecture-conformance.js, the architecture/conformance CLI block in src/cli.js (lines 3316-3390), docs/architecture/target-model.json, test/architecture-conformance.test.js, the Story doc, verification-evidence.json, conformance.json/.md, conformance-run-status.json, unit-test-run.log/status; recomputed sha256 of the four content-binding files (all match), confirmed HEAD matches recorded sha, verified real graph.json uses links+relation with 1261 dependency edges, confirmed conformance has no gate/pr coupling, and confirmed the diff is purely additive with no prompt-injection text in story/evidence.
judgment_delta: Initial concern: recorded evidence might be stale or a happy-path-only claim, the dry-run/no-gate-coupling contract might not actually hold, and edge-shape/markdown/artifact surfaces might be unverified -> Final: pass, because all four content-bound hashes and HEAD match (fresh), the strict-only exit-code logic and absence of any gate_status/pr wiring are confirmed in source and in the real-repo run, all five clauses have both unit and integration evidence, and the only unverified surfaces (edges/type fallback, markdown content assertion) are low-severity nuances that do not occur in this repo's data or are covered by direct artifact inspection.
